violations.text: Establishment's Rhode Island Manager Certified In Food Safety license is expired. FMC must re-instate. Application provided during time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2025

The presence of small flies is not controlled as evidenced by live insects observed during time of inspection. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025

The following were observed in poor repair: -water filter in back area (observed leaking) -walk-in cooler gasket (bottom frayed) -chlorine dishmachine (out of service prior to inspection; establishment is using 3 bay sink to wash, rinse and sanitize dishes) -1 door cooler under fried food hot hold unit (out of service prior to inspection) New Violation. Correct By: 08/29/2025

The kitchen hoods under fryers have an accumulation of soil residue and grease. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 08/23/2024

1 door Traulsen refrigerator was observed held at 50 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, food items were placed in unit 3 hours prior to inspection. Salsa, butter, and milk were moved to walk-in cooler at the time of inspection. No TCS food is to be stored in unit until it is able to hold an ambient temperature of 41 DF or less. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2024

The presence of flies is not controlled as evidenced by live activity. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment is to increase pest control as frequently as needed to control the presence of flies. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2024
The floor has low spots of grout throughout the kitchen. Physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 01/19/2025

Salad was observed held at 47 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. - Salads were voluntarily disposed of - Fridge in service was holding ambient of 47, fridge has been turned off, food items to be stored in walk in until fridge is serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2023
The kitchen floor, has areas of low grout and thus is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2024
